description: Latest-vs-latest cross-framework diffusion benchmark on 4x B200 (b200-verda-k8s): SGLang-Diffusion vs vLLM-Omni vs LightX2V vs TensorRT-LLM VisualGen. Single-request e2e + high-pressure throughput, no cache, torch compile disabled. Cosmos3 benchmarked as text-to-video.

policy.version_policy: latest-vs-latest (sglang origin/main HEAD vs each competitor's newest line)
policy.latency_source: client-side wall clock, unified across frameworks (server-side timers are framework-specific and kept only as sglang diagnostics). single_e2e = median of back-to-back measured requests after warmup (image 5 repeats; video 3 warmups + 2 repeats), not a single isolated request.
policy.single_e2e_anomaly_handling: vLLM-Omni 0.24.0rc1 has an intermittent ~55s concurrency-1 idle/scheduler stall (engine runs in ~1s; confirmed by controlled repeat timing and by historical 0.22 = 5s). Its isolated single-request latencies do not converge or exceed the steady-state throughput p50 by >=1.8x, so the four image cases are marked `anomaly` and their number is withheld; the video cases (real compute >> stall) are unaffected. Throughput (continuous load) never triggers the stall and is clean for all frameworks.
policy.throughput: bench_serving; image 32 req / concurrency 4, video 8 req / concurrency 2
policy.attention_backends: full-precision / exact only, kept semantically consistent across frameworks. sglang uses FA (set_fa_ver(4) on Blackwell); vLLM-Omni uses FlashInfer (blackwell-flashinfer) or FLASH_ATTN where the Hopper kernel loads; LightX2V uses FlashAttention 2 (blackwell-fa2) -- deliberately NOT SageAttention (int8 QK, different numeric class). trtllm-visual uses its default PyTorch-backend attention.
policy.ci_machines: not run on CI/runner/shared automation machines

lightx2v.notes: torch 2.8.0+cu128 isolated venv; flash-attn 2.8.3 (source, TORCH_CUDA_ARCH_LIST=10.0), sageattention 1.0.6, flashinfer-python 0.6.11 installed. FA3/FA4 unavailable on this Blackwell build (Hopper-only FA3 kernel; FA4 CuTe-DSL needs CUDA toolkit >= 13.1, system is 13.0).
lightx2v.label: LightX2V main
